TypeSignature.Value: .class public sequential ansi sealed beforefieldinit SKMatrix extends System.ValueType implements class System.IEquatable`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix>

Docs.summary: A 3x3 transformation matrix with perspective.
Docs.remarks: It extends the traditional 2D affine transformation matrix with three perspective components that allow simple 3D effects to be created with it. Those components must be manually set by using the , , fields of the matrix.

MemberSignature.Value: public SKMatrix (float[] values);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(float32[] values) cil managed

Docs.param: An array of 9 values in row-major order.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct from an array of 9 values in row-major order.

MemberSignature.Value: public SKMatrix (float scaleX, float skewX, float transX, float skewY, float scaleY, float transY, float persp0, float persp1, float persp2);

.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(float32 scaleX, float32 skewX, float32 transX, float32 skewY, float32 scaleY, float32 transY, float32 persp0, float32 persp1, float32 persp2) cil managed

Docs.summary: Creates a new instance of using the specified values.

MemberSignature.Value: public static SkiaSharp.SKMatrix Concat (SkiaSharp.SKMatrix first, SkiaSharp.SKMatrix second);
.method public static hidebysig valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix Concat(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix first, valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix second) cil managed

Docs.summary: Concatenates two matrices and returns the result.

MemberSignature.Value: public static void Concat (ref SkiaSharp.SKMatrix target, SkiaSharp.SKMatrix first, SkiaSharp.SKMatrix second);
.method public static hidebysig void Concat(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix& target, valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix first, valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix second) cil managed

Docs.summary: Concatenates the specified matrices into the resulting target matrix.
Docs.remarks: Either source matrices can also be the target matrix.

Docs.value: Returns if this is an identity matrix; otherwise, .

Docs.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this matrix can be inverted.
Docs.value: Returns if the matrix is invertible; otherwise, .

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapPoint (SkiaSharp.SKPoint point);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapPoint(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint point) cil managed

Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to a point.
Docs.remarks: Mapping points uses all components of the matrix. Use to ignore the translation.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapPoint (float x, float y);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapPoint(float32 x, float32 y) cil managed

Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to a point.
Docs.remarks: Mapping points uses all components of the matrix. Use to ignore the translation.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] MapPoints (SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] points);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] MapPoints(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] points) cil managed

Docs.param: The array of points to be mapped.
Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to an array of points.
Docs.returns: Returns the new array allocated with the mapped results.
Docs.remarks: Mapping points uses all components of the matrix. Use to ignore the translation.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly void MapPoints (SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] result, SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] points);
.method public hidebysig instance void MapPoints(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] result, valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] points) cil managed

Docs.param: The array where the mapped results will be stored (needs to have the same number of elements of the array).
The array of points to be mapped.
Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to an array of points.
Docs.remarks: Mapping points uses all components of the matrix. Use to ignore the translation.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly void MapPoints (Span<SkiaSharp.SKPoint> result, ReadOnlySpan<SkiaSharp.SKPoint> points);
.method public hidebysig instance void MapPoints(valuetype System.Span`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint> result, valuetype System.ReadOnlySpan`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint> points) cil managed

Docs.param: The span where the transformed points will be stored.
The span of points to transform.
Docs.summary: Transforms points using this matrix.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly float MapRadius (float radius);
.method public hidebysig instance float32 MapRadius(float32 radius) cil managed

Docs.summary: Calculates the mean radius of a circle after it has been mapped by this matrix.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKRect MapRect (SkiaSharp.SKRect source);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKRect MapRect(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKRect source) cil managed

Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to a rectangle.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapVector (SkiaSharp.SKPoint vector);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapVector(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint vector) cil managed

Docs.summary: Transforms a vector using this matrix, ignoring translation.
Docs.remarks: Mapping vectors ignores the translation component in the matrix. Use to take the translation into consideration.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapVector (float x, float y);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint MapVector(float32 x, float32 y) cil managed

Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to a vector, ignoring translation.
Docs.remarks: Mapping vectors ignores the translation component in the matrix. Use to take the translation into consideration.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] MapVectors (SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] vectors);
.method public hidebysig instance valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] MapVectors(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] vectors) cil managed

Docs.param: The array of vectors to map.
Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to the array of vectors, ignoring translation, and returns the mapped results.
Docs.returns: Returns the new array allocated with the mapped results.
Docs.remarks: Mapping vectors ignores the translation component in the matrix. Use to take the translation into consideration.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly void MapVectors (SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] result, SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] vectors);
.method public hidebysig instance void MapVectors(valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] result, valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint[] vectors) cil managed

Docs.param: The array where the mapped results will be stored (needs to have the same number of elements of the array).
The array of vectors to map.
Docs.summary: Applies the matrix to the array of vectors and returns the mapped results.
Docs.remarks: Mapping vectors ignores the translation component in the matrix. Use to take the translation into consideration.

MemberSignature.Value: public readonly void MapVectors (Span<SkiaSharp.SKPoint> result, ReadOnlySpan<SkiaSharp.SKPoint> vectors);
.method public hidebysig instance void MapVectors(valuetype System.Span`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint> result, valuetype System.ReadOnlySpan`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKPoint> vectors) cil managed

Docs.param: The span where the transformed vectors will be stored.
The span of vectors to transform.
Docs.summary: Transforms vectors using this matrix, ignoring translation.
Docs.remarks: Mapping vectors ignores the translation component in the matrix. Use to take the translation into consideration.

.method public hidebysig instance bool TryInvert([out] valuetype SkiaSharp.SKMatrix& inverse) cil managed

Docs.param: The destination value to store the inverted matrix if the matrix can be inverted.
Docs.summary: Attempts to invert the matrix, if possible the inverse matrix contains the result.
Docs.returns: if the matrix can be inverted, and the inverse parameter is initialized with the inverted matrix; otherwise, .

MemberSignature.Value: public float[] Values { get; set; }
.property instance float32[] Values

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the matrix as a flat array: [ScaleX, SkewX, TransX, SkewY, ScaleY, TransY, Persp0, Persp1, Persp2].
Docs.value: The matrix as a flat array of 9 values.
